title: Quality Engineer

rationales.taskAutomation: AI can run statistical process control analysis, flag defect-rate anomalies, and draft nonconformance reports end to end, but root-cause investigation on the physical line and supplier audits stay human.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI-assisted SPC, defect-detection vision systems, and quality-analytics platforms are credible and improving fast across manufacturing.
rationales.adoption: Large manufacturers increasingly deploy AI-assisted quality analytics and vision inspection; smaller operations still rely on manual checks.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can run full SPC-monitoring-to-report loops and flag out-of-control processes, but physical root-cause investigation on the shop floor needs an engineer.

tasksAtRisk: Statistical process control monitoring and reporting
Nonconformance report and CAPA documentation drafting
tasksGrowing: Physical root-cause investigation on the production line
Supplier quality audits and on-site process review
Cross-functional quality-system design for new products
salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 17-2199, p25-p75 rounded
insight: AI vision systems and SPC dashboards now catch defects and flag anomalies automatically, but tracing the anomaly back to its physical cause on the line still needs a quality engineer walking the floor.
